reasoning.typography: Cosmica + PP Mori system has personality and discipline, but the headline doesn't feel uniquely ownable—competent execution of a familiar pattern
reasoning.hierarchy: Strong headline dominance, clear focal point with team photos, good visual weight distribution—the hero does proper hierarchical work
reasoning.distinctiveness: Entirely swappable—'stakes/experience matters' could be any consultancy; team photos on colored backgrounds are 2019 SaaS stencil; case study carousel is pure template
reasoning.color: Dark background with cyan/purple/green accent blocks feels calculated-diverse but restrained; red CTA is disciplined at ~3% usage—competent palette, not adventurous
reasoning.density: Confident breathing room in hero, but the uniform card grid below is generic padding discipline—safe, not distinctive
reasoning.voice: IA is pure consultancy stencil: hero claim → case study proof → testimonial. No structural innovation reflecting their actual methodology or story—just templates filled in

winning_patterns: Hero headline has genuine visual presence and does hierarchical work
Team photography treatment (solid color backgrounds, casual portraits) adds human warmth
Case study visuals show actual product work with mobile screens—proof over decoration
Restrained color usage—red CTA stays disciplined, dark background lets content breathe
anti_patterns: Completely generic consultancy IA structure—hero, case study, testimonial is paint-by-numbers
Colored-background team photos are 2019 design agency stencil
Headline 'When stakes are high, experience matters' could be any consultancy—zero specificity
Featured case study carousel with uniform card treatment is pure template behavior
No structural voice—nothing about the layout reflects thoughtbot's actual POV or methodology
